concerns.text: Phase N+1 tasks (T037-T042) introduce a dual-outcome design ('trust_immediate' vs 'authenticity_retrospective') and 'noise_index' metrics that fundamentally alter the dependent variable and model structure defined in spec.md (which mandates a single 'authenticity_score'). This constitutes a silent relaxation of the spec's core research question and a scope violation, as the tasks execute a study the spec never authorized.

Task T009 restricts first-person pronoun extraction to a hardcoded list (I, me, my, etc.) and specific POS tags ('PRP', 'PRP$'). Spec FR-001 mandates extracting 'first-person pronoun counts' generally. This semantic narrowing risks excluding valid first-person pronouns not in the hardcoded list, failing to satisfy the general requirement of FR-001.

Tasks T009-T011 explicitly depend on 'data/raw/conversations.jsonl' as input. No task exists in the plan or tasks list to acquire, download, or generate this specific raw dataset file. This creates a silent failure condition where the extraction pipeline has no data source, violating the requirement for executable data acquisition.

concerns.location: tasks.md:T005, T005a, T017 vs Phase N+1
concerns.text: Tasks T005/T005a validate for a single 'authenticity_score' column. Phase N+1 (T037) requires 'ratings.csv' to contain 'trust_immediate' and 'authenticity_retrospective' instead. This creates a schema mismatch where the foundational validation logic (T005a) will reject the data produced by the later tasks (T037), violating the requirement for a consistent data model.

Task T000 (Power Analysis) references an 'assumed effect size (f²) from literature' but provides no task to locate, cite, or document the specific value used. This violates Constitution Principle II (Verified Accuracy) and SC-005 by leaving a critical statistical parameter undefined and unverified in the execution plan.
